title: Cicerbita putii
taxonomy: Cicerbita putii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is part of the genus Cicerbita, which contains several species of thistle-like plants. Cicerbita putii is native to the Himalayan region and is known for its unique appearance and medicinal properties. The plant is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever and digestive problems. Its classification as a member of the Asteraceae family makes it closely related to other plants in the same family, such as sunflowers and daisies.

intro: Cicerbita putii, also known as Tibetan thistle, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the high-altitude grasslands of the Himalayas and Tibetan Plateau. The plant is valued for its traditional use in Ayurvedic and Tibetan medicine, where it is used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Cicerbita putii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, Cicerbita putii is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to support digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost immunity.
